title: CGM Eligibility Question — Excellent Handling
scenario: Customer asks if they're covered for a Continuous Glucose Monitor. Agent explains criteria clearly and offers next steps.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Thanks for calling UMS, this is Jamie. How can I help?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Hi, my endocrinologist mentioned a continuous glucose monitor and I wanted to see if my insurance would cover it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Great question. I can definitely help you understand your options. Medicare and most insurances cover CGMs when certain criteria are met. Can I ask, are you on insulin currently?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes, I take it multiple times a day.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Perfect — that's one of the main coverage criteria. The next step is your doctor submitting a prescription and a Certificate of Medical Necessity. I can fax the form directly to their office today if you have their fax number.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Oh, I don't know the fax off the top of my head.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: No problem at all. Can I have your doctor's name and clinic? I'll look it up in our system.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Found them. I'll fax the Certificate of Medical Necessity and a welcome packet today. Once they send it back, typical turnaround from start to shipping is about ten business days. I'll call you when everything is approved.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Thank you so much, that was really clear.
